metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit test (unit or live) that asserts uploader identity and trailer content after rebase, e.g., record a fixture where the pre-rebase patchset uploader is UserA and assert the post-rebase patchset from `GET /changes/{id}?o=DETAILED_ACCOUNTS` still shows UserA as uploader and retains the Signed-off-by trailer.
findings.finding: The rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader AC claims uploader and trailer preservation after rebase, but neither the unit-test step nor the live check step is specified to assert on uploader identity or trailer content post-rebase. The unit tests are described only as covering 'FIFO ordering + merge-vs-chain-vs-single routing' — uploader/trailer preservation is not listed as a unit-test target.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — third bullet: 'RebaseInput.rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader=true preserves the uploader + DCO Signed-off-by/rebar-ticket trailer'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command and expected observable evidence to the live check AC (e.g., 'run `pytest tests/live/test_rebase_chain.py -v`; assert HTTP 200 returned by rebase:chain, assert new patchset's uploader equals original uploader via `GET /changes/{id}?o=DETAILED_ACCOUNTS`').
findings.finding: The live check AC has no concrete proving command and no stated observable outcome. It says 'a live check exercises rebase:chain + rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader against the Gerrit 3.14.1 instance' but specifies neither the invocation (script path, command, required environment) nor the expected evidence of success (HTTP status, resulting patchset number, uploader field value, etc.).
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — Tests bullet: 'a live check exercises rebase:chain + rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader against the Gerrit 3.14.1 instance'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the concrete detection method for merge changes (e.g., 'check that `GET /changes/{id}/revisions/current` returns `parents` with length > 1') so the routing condition is fully specified without executor guesswork.
findings.finding: The plan never specifies HOW to detect a merge change for routing purposes. MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE is a RebaseInput field (sent in the body), not a detection mechanism. An executor must independently decide which Gerrit API field or heuristic identifies a change as a merge commit (e.g., checking `parents` count on the revision object, a dedicated Gerrit option like `MERGE_LIST`, or something else).
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — Rebase-routing bullet: 'a `--no-ff` merge change ALWAYS uses POST /changes/{id}/rebase'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Rebase-routing AC: 'Detect a merge change by inspecting the `parents` array on the current revision object; a change is a merge if `len(revision.parents) > 1`.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the concrete merge-change detection predicate in the Rebase-routing AC — e.g. 'detect a merge change by checking that the current revision's `parents` array has length > 1 (via `GET /changes/{id}/revisions/current/commit`, reusing the same pattern as `gerrit_client.get_commit()`)' — so the routing branch condition is fully specified without implementer guesswork.
findings.finding: The rebase-routing AC specifies that a '--no-ff merge change ALWAYS uses POST /changes/{id}/rebase (first-parent-only MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE)' but MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE is a RebaseInput body field (what to send in the POST), not a detection signal. The plan never specifies how loop.py detects that a given change IS a merge commit — e.g. by checking that the revision's `parents` array has length > 1 via GET /changes/{id}/revisions/current/commit. Without the detection step, an implementer has no specification for the routing branch condition, and the existing gerrit_client.py's get_commit() / parents-length pattern is not referenced.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — Rebase-routing bullet: 'a `--no-ff` merge change ALWAYS uses POST /changes/{id}/rebase (first-parent-only `MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE`), NEVER `rebase:chain`'
findings.suggested_fix: Append to the Rebase-routing AC bullet: 'Detect a merge change via `GET /changes/{id}/revisions/current/commit`: if `len(commit.parents) > 1` the change is a merge and must use single `POST /changes/{id}/rebase` with `{"strategy": "MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE"}` in the body. This reuses `gerrit_client.get_commit()`'s existing pattern.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the Files section whether `submit` is stubbed/included in S2a's gerrit.py or intentionally deferred to S2c; if deferred, remove 'submit' from the S2a gerrit.py description.
findings.finding: The plan's Files section states gerrit.py covers 'submit' calls, but POST /changes/{id}/submit is the S2c deliverable (ancestor-atomic submit). This creates a scope ambiguity: is the submit endpoint implemented in S2a's gerrit.py or added later in S2c? If gerrit.py is built without submit in S2a, S2c must edit gerrit.py (an unenumerated edit to an S2a artifact); if submit IS in-scope for S2a, S2a carries behavior owned by S2c.
findings.location: Files section — infra/autolander/gerrit.py description; AC: Tests

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the explicit prerequisite for the live check AC (e.g., 'S5 must be merged first, OR a manually created SSM parameter `/autolander/dev/gerrit_password` can be used for local live-check runs') so the executor knows when this AC becomes satisfiable.
findings.finding: The live check AC depends on credentials 'provisioned by S5' with no fallback or in-session setup path described. If S5 is not yet complete, the live check cannot be run, making this AC potentially not in-session completable for an executor working only on this ticket.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — Gerrit helper bullet and Tests bullet; 'provisioned by S5'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Dependencies' section or a note on the Tests AC: 'Prerequisite: S5 merged (SSM parameter populated); for dev runs, manually populate `/autolander/gerrit/dev/http_password` in SSM.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a retry/backoff policy (e.g., 3 attempts with exponential backoff) and a per-request timeout to the Gerrit helper, and specify how persistent failures are surfaced (exception propagated to the loop, which logs and backs off).
findings.finding: The plan specifies no retry, backoff, timeout, or circuit-breaker for any of the Gerrit API calls (GET /changes, GET related, POST rebase, POST rebase:chain, POST submit). A slow or transiently unavailable Gerrit instance could block the loop indefinitely or silently drop work.
findings.location: Gerrit helper (infra/autolander/gerrit.py) + Acceptance Criteria — no mention of retry/backoff/timeout anywhere
findings.suggested_fix: In gerrit.py, wrap all urllib calls with a configurable timeout (e.g., 30 s) and a retry decorator (up to 3 attempts, 1-2-4 s backoff). Let the loop catch the terminal exception, log it with the change-id, and sleep before re-entering selection.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a named config artifact to the Files section (e.g., `infra/autolander/config.py` or inline dataclass in `gerrit.py`) that reads `AUTOLANDER_GERRIT_TOKEN` and `GERRIT_BASE_URL` from the environment — mirroring the `ReceiverConfig.from_env()` pattern without importing `src/rebar`.
findings.finding: The plan never enumerates a config module or object for infra/autolander/gerrit.py. The existing GerritClient (src/rebar/review_bot/gerrit_client.py) takes a ReceiverConfig from src/rebar, but the plan explicitly forbids importing from src/rebar. The new gerrit.py must authenticate as RebarBotNava using an SSM-backed token (AUTOLANDER_GERRIT_TOKEN, per S5b), but no config dataclass, env-var reading pattern, or config file is named in the Files section or ACs.
findings.location: Files section — infra/autolander/gerrit.py; AC: Gerrit helper
findings.suggested_fix: Add `infra/autolander/config.py` to the Files section with a simple `dataclass AutolanderConfig` with `gerrit_base_url: str`, `bot_user: str = 'RebarBotNava'`, `gerrit_bot_token: str`, and a `from_env()` classmethod reading `GERRIT_BASE_URL` and `AUTOLANDER_GERRIT_TOKEN`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add test file paths to the Files section (e.g. `infra/autolander/tests/test_loop.py` for unit tests and `infra/autolander/tests/test_gerrit_live.py` for the live check) so every AC has a named, inspectable deliverable.
findings.finding: The Tests AC describes concrete test deliverables (unit tests + a live check), but no test file path appears in the Files section. This makes the Tests AC an orphan criterion with no covered deliverable, violating the bidirectional coverage requirement.
findings.location: Files section (lists only `infra/autolander/loop.py` and `infra/autolander/gerrit.py`) vs. Tests AC ('unit-test the FIFO ordering + the merge-vs-chain-vs-single routing against recorded Gerrit JSON; a live check exercises rebase:chain')
findings.suggested_fix:
Append to Files:
- `infra/autolander/tests/test_loop.py` — unit tests: FIFO ordering, merge/chain/single routing against recorded Gerrit JSON fixtures.
- `infra/autolander/tests/test_gerrit_live.py` — live integration check: rebase:chain + rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader against Gerrit 3.14.1 (requires S5).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an ADR or CLAUDE.md entry capturing the decision to keep `infra/autolander/gerrit.py` stdlib-only and independent of `src/rebar`, explaining the rationale and the maintenance contract (which calls each helper owns).
findings.finding: The decision to create a new parallel stdlib-only Gerrit helper (`infra/autolander/gerrit.py`) instead of importing from `src/rebar/review_bot/gerrit_client.py` is a non-trivial architectural decision that is justified only inline in the plan, with no ADR, CLAUDE.md entry, or design-doc update planned.
findings.location: ## Files — `infra/autolander/gerrit.py` description; AC — Gerrit helper bullet
findings.suggested_fix: Add a short ADR (e.g., `docs/adr/0NNN-autolander-stdlib-gerrit-helper.md`) or a `## Architecture` section in `infra/autolander/README.md` covering: (1) why gerrit.py is not an import of src/rebar, (2) which Gerrit API calls each helper owns, (3) how to keep them from diverging (e.g., shared test fixtures, or a note that gerrit.py only covers autolander-needed endpoints).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly acknowledge the dual-Gerrit-helper coupling risk in the plan (or ADR) and specify a mitigation: e.g., a shared low-level urllib utility, a stated ownership split by endpoint namespace, or a periodic sync check in CI.
findings.finding: The plan introduces two parallel Gerrit helper implementations (`infra/autolander/gerrit.py` and `src/rebar/review_bot/gerrit_client.py`) without acknowledging the coupling/divergence risk or specifying a mitigation strategy.
findings.location: ## Files — `infra/autolander/gerrit.py`; AC — Gerrit helper bullet

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add test cases (unit or live) for: empty Autosubmit queue, Gerrit 409/conflict on rebase, HTTP 4xx/5xx from the helper, and a change losing submittability between selection and rebase call.
findings.finding: The test plan covers only happy paths: FIFO ordering with valid JSON and successful rebase routing. No failure, error, or empty-queue scenarios are specified — e.g., empty Autosubmit queue, Gerrit returning 409 (rebase conflict), 404, 5xx, network timeout, or a change becoming non-submittable between selection and rebase. For a new user-facing auto-lander loop, these are operationally critical paths.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — Tests bullet
findings.suggested_fix: Extend the Tests AC: 'unit-test the FIFO ordering + the merge-vs-chain-vs-single routing against recorded Gerrit JSON, **including recorded error responses (409 conflict, 5xx, empty result set)**; assert the helper raises/returns the correct exception type and that the loop skips or back-offs appropriately; a live check exercises rebase:chain + rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader against the Gerrit 3.14.1 instance.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether `infra/autolander/` runs in an environment where `src/rebar` is installable; if so, extend `GerritClient` (or extract a minimal shared base) rather than rebuilding the urllib+XSSI+Basic-auth scaffold a third time — or add an explicit architectural rationale (e.g., 'autolander container has no rebar package') to justify the isolation.
findings.finding: The plan builds a new infra/autolander/gerrit.py that duplicates the urllib+Basic-auth+XSSI scaffold already in production at src/rebar/review_bot/gerrit_client.py (4+ import sites: voter.py, app.py, reconcile.py, tests/unit/test_review_bot.py), producing a third copy of _strip_xssi logic (mirror_guard.py already carries a second copy, self-documented as 'mirrors review_bot.gerrit_client._strip_xssi'). The plan's stated rationale is 'no import of src/rebar', but it does not confirm whether this isolation is a hard deployment constraint (separate container, different Python env) or a stylistic preference. If the autolander process has access to the installed rebar package, this is NIH rebuild of production functionality rather than a justified architecture boundary.
findings.location: Files section: 'infra/autolander/gerrit.py — thin STDLIB-ONLY Gerrit helper (the src/rebar/review_bot/gerrit_client.py urllib+Basic-auth+XSSI pattern; no new dependency, no import of src/rebar)'
findings.suggested_fix: If the autolander is a standalone script/container without the rebar package: document this hard constraint explicitly in the plan. If it shares the same Python env: add the new Gerrit calls (RelatedChanges, rebase, rebase:chain) as methods on the existing GerritClient class, letting infra/autolander/loop.py import GerritClient directly — no new file needed.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `infra/autolander/__init__.py` to the Files section (even if empty) to make the directory a proper Python package.
findings.finding: The plan names no `infra/autolander/__init__.py`. The `infra/autolander/` directory does not currently exist; creating `loop.py` and `gerrit.py` inside it without an `__init__.py` means the directory is not a Python package and imports between the two files (e.g., `from infra.autolander.gerrit import ...`) will fail at runtime unless the module is run as a script rather than imported.
findings.location: Files section — infra/autolander/

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief positive rationale for the `ApprovalInfo.date` FIFO key in the Selection AC — e.g. 'vote-date captures the agent's intent-to-land instant rather than upload order, so a later-uploaded change that was voted first is correctly prioritised; note that a re-vote resets the key and moves the change to the back of the queue (intentional).'
findings.finding: The plan states no positive rationale for choosing ApprovalInfo.date (the Autosubmit vote timestamp) as the FIFO key rather than available alternatives such as change._number (submission-order proxy), change.created (upload time), or a separate queue-entry time. The 'why this key' is load-bearing for correctness (two agents can vote Autosubmit in an order uncorrelated with change number) but is left implicit.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — Selection bullet: 'FIFO keyed on the `Autosubmit` vote's `ApprovalInfo.date`'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Evaluate whether the shared urllib+Basic-auth+XSSI transport layer can be factored into a small internal module (e.g. `infra/autolander/_gerrit_http.py`) to satisfy the 'no src/rebar import' constraint without wholesale-duplicating the existing gerrit_client.py boilerplate; if full duplication is chosen anyway, document why extraction was rejected.
findings.finding: The plan creates a brand-new parallel Gerrit HTTP helper (infra/autolander/gerrit.py) replicating the same urllib+Basic-auth+XSSI boilerplate already in src/rebar/review_bot/gerrit_client.py without evaluating whether the shared low-level transport layer could be extracted into an internal shared module (avoiding full duplication) rather than a wholesale second copy.
findings.location: Files section / Gerrit helper AC / 'the src/rebar/review_bot/gerrit_client.py urllib+Basic-auth+XSSI pattern'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete citation (Gerrit 3.14.1 REST API doc URL or endpoint path as shown in the live instance's `/Documentation/` page) for the `rebase:chain` endpoint confirmation, so the capability assertion is verifiable from the plan itself.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'POST /changes/{id}/rebase:chain confirmed on this instance's docs' but provides no citation, link, or recorded evidence of that confirmation against the live Gerrit 3.14.1 instance. If the endpoint path or its availability is wrong, the core routing AC silently fails.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — Rebase-routing AC: 'POST /changes/{id}/rebase:chain (confirmed on this instance's docs; linear ancestry only, no committer_email)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the AC or a 'References' section: the Gerrit REST API doc path, e.g. `GET https://<instance>/Documentation/rest-api-changes.html#rebase-chain`, confirming the endpoint exists and its exact path on Gerrit 3.14.1.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify and cite that `RebaseInput.rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader=true` (a) exists in Gerrit 3.14.1's REST API and (b) preserves the commit message trailers (not just the Gerrit uploader field); add a test fixture or Gerrit doc reference confirming this behaviour before the plan is committed to this approach.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'RebaseInput.rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader=true preserves the uploader + DCO Signed-off-by/rebar-ticket trailer' without citing evidence that this RebaseInput field is available and behaves as described on Gerrit 3.14.1 specifically (it was introduced in a particular Gerrit version and its trailer-preservation semantics are version-dependent).
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — third bullet: 'RebaseInput.rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader=true preserves the uploader + DCO Signed-off-by/rebar-ticket trailer'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] **[T5c/transit]** Declare that the autolander's Gerrit base URL MUST use HTTPS (or a Unix-socket/loopback TLS-terminated path), and add a startup assertion in `gerrit.py` (or the loop config) that rejects an `http://` scheme to prevent the SSM-provisioned bot token from being sent in plaintext.
findings.finding: The plan instructs the new gerrit.py helper to 'reuse this repo's STDLIB-ONLY pattern (urllib+Basic-auth)' but never explicitly requires HTTPS for the autolander's Gerrit base URL. HTTP Basic auth sends credentials base64-encoded over the wire; if the configured base URL is http:// rather than https://, the bot's SSM-backed token is exposed in plaintext on the network path.
findings.location: Gerrit helper AC — 'reuses this repo's STDLIB-ONLY pattern (urllib+Basic-auth+XSSI pattern)'
findings.suggested_fix: In gerrit.py's `__init__` (or an equivalent config-validation step), add: `if not self._base.startswith('https://'): raise ValueError(f'AUTOLANDER_GERRIT_BASE_URL must use https://; got {self._base!r}')`. Also declare in the AC or config docs that the env var must be an https:// URL.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify the S5 sequencing dependency: either document that the live-check AC requires S5 to have provisioned the `RebarBotNava` SSM password first (and update the 'loadable whole' claim accordingly), or describe a fallback credential setup so the live test is truly self-contained.
findings.finding: The plan claims the unit is 'loadable whole' (split out 'to keep each unit loadable whole'), yet the live-check AC depends on credentials provisioned by a *different* plan unit (S5). If S5 has not yet run, the live test cannot pass, creating a sequencing contradiction between the stated independence goal and the actual runtime dependency.
findings.location: Why section ('keep each unit loadable whole') vs. Tests AC ('live check exercises rebase:chain … against the Gerrit 3.14.1 instance') vs. Gerrit helper AC ('SSM-backed HTTP password (provisioned by S5)')
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Depends on' line (e.g. 'Depends on: S5 for RebarBotNava HTTP-password SSM parameter') and soften 'loadable whole' to 'loadable for unit tests; live check requires S5 credentials'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an observability AC: specify that each Gerrit call outcome (success/failure/retry-exhausted), routing decision (single/chain/merge), and rebase result is emitted as a structured log entry, and that terminal failures increment a named metric or trigger an alert.
findings.finding: No observability is specified for the new failure points introduced by this plan — Gerrit call failures, SSM credential fetch failures, routing-decision outcomes, and rebase results are not instrumented with any log, metric, or alert.
findings.location: Files section (loop.py, gerrit.py) + Acceptance Criteria — no observability AC or mention of logging/metrics

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how the loop detects and handles partial rebase:chain failure on retry (e.g., re-read current revision/parent before issuing a second rebase, skip if already on correct base, or rely on Gerrit's idempotency guarantee and document it explicitly).
findings.finding: Rebase and rebase:chain are mutating writes, but the plan specifies no idempotency or recovery strategy for partial failure — if rebase:chain partially rebases a chain (some changes updated, then the call fails), re-running the selection loop may attempt to rebase again from a different base, corrupting chain order or causing duplicate rebase events.
findings.location: AC: Rebase-routing (POST /changes/{id}/rebase:chain) + AC: RebaseInput.rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define how mutual exclusion is enforced across loop instances (e.g., a single-instance deployment constraint, a Gerrit-side optimistic-lock by checking current revision before rebasing, or a distributed lock) so that two concurrent selectors cannot both mutate the same front change.
findings.finding: The plan does not address concurrent loop instances: if two instances run simultaneously (crash-restart, rolling deploy), both may select the same front change and independently issue rebase or rebase:chain, causing a TOCTOU race between selection and the mutating write.
findings.location: infra/autolander/loop.py — selection + rebase-routing; no locking/singleton mechanism mentioned
findings.suggested_fix: Before issuing rebase/rebase:chain, re-fetch the change's current revision and compare it to the one read during selection; if it has advanced (another instance already rebased it), skip and re-enter selection. Document this as the optimistic-concurrency guard.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify and correct the bot identity claim: identify which Gerrit service user (`RebarBotNava` vs `rebar-review-bot` vs a new account) the autolander will authenticate as, confirm an SSM slot and provisioning script exist for that identity's HTTP password, and fix the 'provisioned by S5' dependency to name the correct upstream story.
findings.finding: The plan asserts the autolander bot authenticates as `RebarBotNava` via an SSM-backed HTTP password 'provisioned by S5', but grep evidence refutes both sub-claims: (a) `RebarBotNava` is NOT a provisioned service user with an HTTP password — it only appears as the default `CONTRIBUTOR_MEMBERS` value in `setup-project.sh`; the repo's actual bot service user is `rebar-review-bot` (wired end-to-end in `service-user.sh`, `config.py`, `smoke-check.sh`, `reviewbot-e2e.sh`, and SSM slot `/rebar/prod/gerrit-bot-token`); (b) the existing 'S5' in this codebase is the Gerrit→GitHub replication story (`monitoring_s5.tf` tags `Story = 'S5'`, `setup-replication.sh` header 'Story S5', `replication.config` 'Story S5'), not any bot-credential-provisioning story — so the 'provisioned by S5' dependency points to the wrong upstream story.
findings.location: Gerrit helper AC — 'the bot authenticates as `RebarBotNava` via its SSM-backed HTTP password (provisioned by S5)'
findings.suggested_fix: If the autolander should reuse the existing `rebar-review-bot` identity and its `/rebar/prod/gerrit-bot-token` SSM slot (already fetched into `GERRIT_BOT_TOKEN`), update the AC to name `rebar-review-bot` and reference the existing provisioning (S4a / `service-user.sh`). If a separate `RebarBotNava` identity is required, a new service-user provisioning step (a new story, not S5) must create the Gerrit account, generate an HTTP token, and store it in a new SSM slot before this plan can depend on it.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State an explicit pre-condition (or `depends_on`) for the `Autosubmit` Gerrit label to exist in `project.config` before this plan's live-check AC can pass, or add label provisioning (the `[label "Autosubmit"]` block in `infra/gerrit/project.config` and the matching ACL in `setup-project.sh`) to this plan's scope.
findings.finding: The FIFO selection query `label:Autosubmit+1 is:submittable` depends on an `Autosubmit` Gerrit label that is NOT provisioned in the live instance's `project.config`. The label is absent from every authoritative config file in the repo; its only repo appearance outside cache files is an eval-spec carve-out note that explicitly marks it 'a symbol not yet in the codebase'. If the label doesn't exist, the query returns zero results and the autolander's selection step is a no-op against the live Gerrit.
findings.location: AC — Selection: `GET /changes/?q=label:Autosubmit+1 is:submittable&o=DETAILED_LABELS`
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the plan's ## Dependencies or ## Pre-conditions section: 'Requires the `Autosubmit` label to be provisioned in `infra/gerrit/project.config` (e.g. `function = NoBlock; value = 0 No score; value = +1 Autosubmit`) and the `label-Autosubmit = -1..+1 group Service Users` ACL — either inherited from the parent plan S2/perovskite-frilly-grebe (list it as depends_on) or added to this plan's Files scope.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the Gerrit helper AC to name `rebar-review-bot` (not `RebarBotNava`) as the authenticating service user and `/rebar/prod/gerrit-bot-token` (not 'provisioned by S5') as the SSM slot; if the autolander needs a *distinct* bot identity, state explicitly that a new SSM slot and service-user.sh invocation are in scope.
findings.finding: The Gerrit helper AC asserts 'the bot authenticates as `RebarBotNava` via its SSM-backed HTTP password (provisioned by S5)'. Both sub-claims are contradicted by repo evidence: (a) `RebarBotNava` is only the default `CONTRIBUTOR_MEMBERS` value in `setup-project.sh` — it is NOT a provisioned Gerrit service user with an HTTP token; the actual autolander-eligible bot service user is `rebar-review-bot` (wired in `service-user.sh`, `smoke-check.sh`, `reviewbot-e2e.sh`, and SSM slot `/rebar/prod/gerrit-bot-token`); (b) 'S5' in this repo tags the GitHub replication story (`monitoring_s5.tf Story='S5'`, `setup-replication.sh` header 'Story S5'), not any bot-credential provisioning story.
findings.location: AC — Gerrit helper: 'the bot authenticates as `RebarBotNava` via its SSM-backed HTTP password (provisioned by S5)'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'the bot authenticates as `RebarBotNava` via its SSM-backed HTTP password (provisioned by S5)' with 'the bot authenticates as `rebar-review-bot` via SSM slot `/rebar/prod/gerrit-bot-token` (the existing bot token, already provisioned by the review-bot infrastructure)' — or, if a separate autolander identity is desired, add service-user provisioning to this plan's scope.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify (via the live-check AC or a SPIKE) that `rebar-review-bot` holds the `Rebase On Behalf Of Others` global capability on this Gerrit instance, and if not, add its provisioning (e.g. a `[capability] rebaseOnBehalfOfOthers = group Service Users` block in `infra/gerrit/project.config` via `setup-project.sh`) to this plan's scope.
findings.finding: The `rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader=true` field in `RebaseInput` is asserted as a working capability on this Gerrit 3.14.1 instance, but it has never been exercised in any existing repo script, fixture, or e2e test. The field was introduced in Gerrit 3.8, so version compatibility is not the issue, but there is no repo-local proof that the live instance's ACLs actually permit a service user to invoke `rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader` (this requires the bot to have the `Rebase On Behalf Of Others` global capability or equivalent). The plan does not verify this precondition.
findings.location: AC — `RebaseInput.rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader=true` preserves the uploader + DCO `Signed-off-by`/`rebar-ticket` trailer
findings.suggested_fix: Add to `infra/gerrit/project.config` under a `[access]` block for the global capability namespace: `rebaseOnBehalfOfOthers = group Service Users`, and update `setup-project.sh` to push the change. Document this as a required precondition in the AC.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
